race.name: La Drômoise Gravel
race.tagline: Vineyard passages and Vercors cliff views through the Drôme and Bez valleys of France.

vitals.date_specific: 2026: Saturday, September 19 (Gravel day, within the Fri Sep 18-Sun Sep 20 Drômoise weekend) CONFIRMED official (cyclodromoise.com)
vitals.field_size: ~3,000-3,600 across the full 3-day Drômoise weekend (all disciplines); gravel-specific count not published (4th gravel edition, 2 courses)
vitals.start_time: 9:30 AM (40km course); Gravel-70km-GPS start time not separately published
vitals.aid_stations: 2 aid stations (km 9 and km 48) on the 70km course; 1 aid station on the 40km course

climate.description: Late-September pre-Alpine conditions: mild days, cool mornings, variable mountain weather.

terrain.primary: Diois forest fire roads (pistes forestières) in the Vercors foothills
terrain.surface: Nearly exclusively forest fire roads; small paved road passages and stony technical sections; GPS-trace navigation on the 70km course (not fully marked)
features: Solaure plateau and Roanne valley forest tracks
Views toward the Dévoluy, Vercors massif, and Mont Ventoux
Clairette vineyard above Aurel and Barsac
Finish near the Monastery of Sainte Croix
Col de Royer split point (Gravel-40 shares its opener with the MTB event, then diverges here)

gravel_god_rating.score_note: Regional France event through the Diois/Vercors foothills — a randonnée-style gravel day (no timing or podium) with a medium local field and a vineyard-to-forest scenic challenge.

biased_opinion.summary: La Drômoise Gravel is a regional randonnée through the Diois forest fire roads and Vercors foothills above Die — vineyard passages, views toward the Vercors massif, and a GPS-trace-only 70km course with no timing or podium. It's a genuine regional gravel day, not a destination race.
strengths: Diois/Vercors foothills scenery — vineyard tracks above Aurel and Barsac, views toward the Vercors massif
Randonnée format — no timing, no podium pressure, just the ride
GPS-trace navigation on real forest fire roads
A 40km marked Lite option for riders who want a shorter day
weaknesses: Field size not published — hard to gauge how busy the course will feel
Regional organization, not an international destination race
European travel costs for non-local riders
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A solid regional Diois/Vercors gravel randonnée. Worth it if you're local to France/Europe or want a low-pressure GPS-navigation day on real forest fire roads. Not worth traveling internationally for.

final_verdict.one_liner: A Diois/Vercors foothills gravel randonnée — GPS-trace navigation on forest fire roads above Die.
final_verdict.should_you_race: If you're local to France/Europe and want a low-key, GPS-navigation randonnée through vineyard tracks and Vercors foothills forest roads — yes, it's solid. If you're traveling internationally for a marquee gravel race, look elsewhere; this is a regional day, not a destination event.
final_verdict.alternatives: For better France races: Gravel Fever, Wish One Millau.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Drôme has limited lodging. Consider nearby cities.

history.origin_story: Organized by Die Cycling Club as part of the wider La Drômoise cyclosportive weekend (Friday-Sunday) in the Diois region of the Drôme. Gravel became its own category in its 4th year, run on Saturday as a standalone GPS-navigation day through the Diois forest fire roads and Vercors foothills.
notable_moments: 19th edition of the wider La Drômoise cyclosportive weekend; gravel is now in its 4th year as its own category
history.reputation: A regional Drôme cyclosportive weekend (Friday-Sunday) with gravel as one of its categories — GPS-trace navigation, forest fire roads, and vineyard passages through the Diois and Vercors foothills, run without timing or podium.

course_description.character: A randonnée through the Diois forest fire roads and Vercors foothills above Die: vineyard tracks above Aurel and Barsac, forest tracks across the Solaure plateau and Roanne valley, and a finish near the Monastery of Sainte Croix. The 70km course runs GPS-trace navigation only — no course marking — with two aid stations along the way. No timing, no podium.

suffering_zones.label: Solaure Plateau Opener
suffering_zones.desc: The course heads out from Die's Meyrosse start onto the Solaure plateau and Roanne valley forest tracks — GPS-trace navigation from the first pedal stroke, with no markings to follow.

suffering_zones.label: First Aid Station
suffering_zones.desc: The first of two aid stations on the 70km course, after the early forest-road navigation settles in.

suffering_zones.label: Col de Royer Split
suffering_zones.desc: The 70km route shares its opener with the 40km Lite course and the MTB event before diverging here — riders on the shorter option peel off while the 70km continues into the Vercors foothills.

suffering_zones.label: Vineyard Tracks Above Aurel and Barsac
suffering_zones.desc: Clairette vineyard tracks above Aurel and Barsac, with views toward the Vercors massif and the Dévoluy — a scenic stretch before the final push.

suffering_zones.label: Second Aid Station
suffering_zones.desc: The second and final aid station on the 70km course, before the closing kilometers toward the finish near the Monastery of Sainte Croix.

course_description.signature_challenge: GPS-trace navigation on unmarked Diois forest fire roads — the terrain is manageable, but finding your way through 70km of forest track without course markings is the real test.

guide_variables.race_terrain: Diois forest fire roads, Vercors foothills

non_negotiables.why: Late-September Diois/Vercors foothills weather is variable — cool mornings, mild afternoons, and possible early-autumn rain.
1,500m of climbing on Diois forest fire roads through the Vercors foothills requires sustained climbing fitness.
GPS-trace-only navigation on the 70km course puts route-finding on the rider, with just two aid stations along the way.

migration_notes: 2026-07-13 wave-24 recon: RECONCILED to official cyclodromoise.com. Real event = 19th La Drômoise cyclosportive weekend, Fri Sep 18-Sun Sep 20 2026, organized by Die Cycling Club (~3,000-3,600 across all 3 days/disciplines). Gravel is its own category (4th gravel edition) on Saturday Sep 19 2026 CONFIRMED, two courses: Gravel-40km/950m (marked, shares MTB opener, splits at Col de Royer) and Gravel-70km-GPS/1,500m (GPS-trace navigation only, forest fire roads, aid stations km9/km48). Anchored vitals on the 70km/1,500m course as flagship (43mi/4,921ft); 40km/950m (25mi/3,117ft) is the Lite alternative, not yet added as a separate keyed course. Corrected: location Die (not generic 'Drôme'), terrain from fabricated 'French Alps' to actual Diois/Vercors foothills pre-Alps, prior stale date '2025: September 18' and prior distance/elevation (62mi/5,500ft matched no real course) fixed. RUNWAY: from 2026-07-16 to 2026-09-19 = ~9.3 weeks -> SHORT ladder (F8/TC8/SMR6), NOT full 7-plan ladder; bank 12wk copy per standing precedent. UNFIXED (out of recon scope, flagged for copy-authoring): biased_opinion/final_verdict/history/course_description blocks below are still 100% generic 'French Alps' template boilerplate (score 49/tier 3) with zero race-specific content -- a real copy-authoring pass using the terrain.features/guide_variables specifics above would meaningfully improve this profile. Field size and cutoff time are NOT publicly available anywhere web-searched -- do not fabricate; use soft/no-number language in copy. Sources: cyclodromoise.com, cyclodromoise.com/project/gravel-70km-gps/, cyclodromoise.com/project/gravel-40km/, finishers.com/en/event/la-dromoise.

topics.pacing: Pacing Diois fire-road climbs and GPS-trace navigation
topics.fueling: Nutrition between two aid stations (km 9 and km 48) on the 70km course
topics.mountains: Sustained climbing strategy for the Vercors foothills
topics.weather: Riding in variable late-September pre-Alpine weather

marketplace_variables.race_hook: Unmarked GPS-trace gravel through the Diois — 70 km of fire-road climbing out of Die.

logistics.explanation: Saturday-morning start from Die's Meyrosse start area, with the 40km Lite option sharing its opener with the MTB event before splitting at Col de Royer. The 70km course runs on GPS-trace navigation only — riders get the GPX trace ahead of race day and are on their own to follow it, with just two aid stations (km 9 and km 48) along the way.

length.explanation: Two gravel distances run on race Saturday: a marked 40km/950m Lite option and the flagship 70km/1,500m GPS-navigation course. At 43 miles with 4,921ft of climbing, the 70km sits solidly in middle-distance territory — a real day out without being an epic suffer-fest.

technicality.explanation: Nearly the entire 70km course runs on Diois forest fire roads, with small paved-road passages and stony technical sections mixed in. GPS-trace navigation adds a route-finding element on top of the terrain itself — riders need to handle both the bike and the map.

elevation.explanation: 1,500m of climbing over 70km through the Diois forest fire roads and Vercors foothills — respectable elevation gain for the distance, delivered through pre-Alps foothill terrain rather than high-mountain passes.

climate.explanation: Late-September conditions in the Diois/Vercors foothills bring mild days, cool mornings, and variable mountain weather, with the possibility of early-autumn rain. Foothill terrain keeps conditions less extreme than high-altitude mountain racing, but layers are still worth packing.

altitude.explanation: The course runs through Vercors foothills terrain — pre-Alps, not high Alps — so altitude isn't a major physiological factor. Expect rolling hill-country climbing rather than thin-air effects.

adventure.explanation: The 70km route strings together the Solaure plateau and Roanne valley forest tracks, vineyard passages above Aurel and Barsac, and views toward the Dévoluy and Vercors massif, finishing near the Monastery of Sainte Croix. GPS-trace navigation on unmarked forest fire roads makes it feel like real exploration rather than a marked course.

prestige.explanation: La Drômoise is a regional French cyclosportive weekend — its gravel category is in its 4th year, run alongside the longer-running road and MTB editions. It's a well-organized regional event rather than a race with international name recognition.

race_quality.explanation: Organized by Die Cycling Club as part of the broader La Drômoise weekend, with a marked 40km Lite option and a GPS-navigation 70km flagship course, two aid stations, and a randonnée format with no timing or podium.

experience.explanation: A randonnée-style day through the Diois forest fire roads and Vercors foothills — no timing, no podium, just the ride. The atmosphere fits a regional French cyclosportive weekend: low-pressure, scenic, and focused on the terrain rather than the results.

community.explanation: Organized by Die Cycling Club within the wider La Drômoise weekend, drawing a regional French field. It's a community event first — the randonnée format (no timing, no rankings) keeps the focus on riding together rather than racing each other.

field_depth.explanation: No timing, no rankings — La Drômoise Gravel runs as a randonnée. Gravel-specific field size isn't published, and the format spreads riders across two courses (40km and 70km) plus the wider weekend's road and MTB events, which thins out any sense of a competitive field.

value.explanation: Entry runs roughly €70-90 through finishers.com for a supported GPS-navigation day with two aid stations, plus the option to drop to the marked 40km Lite course. Reasonable pricing for a regional French cyclosportive weekend.

expenses.explanation: Registration runs €70-90. Die sits in provincial Drôme, roughly two hours from Lyon (LYS) by car — no major airport nearby, so budget for driving or connections through Lyon. Regional lodging costs rather than resort or destination-race pricing.

key_takeaways: 2024 included new parcours like Friday in Baronnies and Ultra on Saturday/Sunday

primary.name: IRC Boken Double Cross
primary.why: Underrated mixed-terrain tire. Fast center with confident shoulder grip.

primary.name: Specialized Pathfinder Pro
primary.why: Fast center, grippy edges. The modern all-rounder benchmark.

primary.name: Vittoria Terreno Mix
primary.why: Graphene versatility. Mixed-terrain grip with reasonable rolling speed.

front_rear_split.rationale: Faster front for reduced rolling resistance on straights, grippier rear for traction on loose climbs and corners

videos.title: “La Corima Drôme Provençale 2022” Official aftermovie 🎬
videos.description:
As a major partner, we are happy to organize alongside the Saint James Vélo Club Montélimar such a beautiful and famous cyclosportive🇫🇷 
 
Relive in pictures this 2022 edition, sponsored by @thomas voeckler, with nearly 2350 participants on 7 different races, from Road to Gravel, for amateurs and passionate people 🚴🏼♂️ 
 
Thank you to everyone involved in this event and see you next year! 📅🔥 
 
🎥 Peignée verticale
